description: county seat of Vanderburgh County, Indiana, United States
description_wiki: Evansville is a city in Vanderburgh County, Indiana, United States, and its county seat.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: 1942-1943 series of battles fought on and around Guadalcanal as part of World War II
description_wiki: The Guadalcanal campaign, also known as the Battle of Guadalcanal and codenamed Operation Watchtower by the United States, was an Allied offensive against forces of the Empire of Japan in the Solomon Islands during the Pacific Theater of World War II. It was fought between 7 August 1942 and 9 February 1943, and involved major land and naval battles on and surrounding the island of Guadalcanal.

description: 1945 major battle in which US Forces captured the island of Iwo Jima from Japan during World War II
description_wiki: The Battle of Iwo Jima (硫黄島の戦い, Iōtō no Tatakai, Iōjima no Tatakai; 19 February – 26 March 1945) was a major battle in which the United States Marine Corps (USMC) and United States Navy (USN) landed on and eventually captured the island of Iwo Jima from the Imperial Japanese Army (IJA) during World War II. The American invasion, designated Operation Detachment, had the goal of capturing the island with its two airfields: South Field and Central Field.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: county seat of Jefferson County, Kentucky, United States
description_wiki: Louisville is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Kentucky, sixth-most populous city in the Southeast, and the 27th-most-populous city in the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Alaska (  ə-LASS-kə) is a non-contiguous U.S. state located in the northwestern regions of North America.

description: United States Army post in Kentucky, United States
description_wiki: Fort Knox is a United States Army installation in Kentucky, south of Louisville and north of Elizabethtown.

description: 7.62 mm automatic rifle
description_wiki: The M14 rifle is an American battle rifle chambered for the 7.62×51mm NATO cartridge.

description: city in and county seat of Fayette County, Kentucky, United States
description_wiki: Lexington is a consolidated city coterminous with Fayette County, Kentucky, United States, of which it is also the county seat.

description: census-designated place in Pulaski County, Missouri, United States
description_wiki: Fort Leonard Wood is a U.S. Army training installation located in the Missouri Ozarks.

description: battalion of the United States Marine Corps
description_wiki: 2nd Combat Engineer Battalion is a combat engineer battalion of the United States Marine Corps .

description: demilitarized zone in Korea
description_wiki: The Korean Demilitarized Zone (Korean: 한반도/조선반도 비무장 지대), also known as simply the Korean DMZ or KDMZ, is a strip of land running across the Korean Peninsula which intersects the 38th parallel north.

description: Sovereign state in East Asia
description_wiki: North Korea, officially the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK), is a country in East Asia.

description: country in East Asia
description_wiki: South Korea, officially the Republic of Korea (ROK), is a country in East Asia.

surface_forms: Pueblo, which was taken off the coast of north Korea

description: ground object that impedes animals but allows humans and vehicles to pass
military award presented to members of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The Meritorious Service Medal (MSM) is a military award presented to members of the United States Armed Forces who distinguish themselves with outstanding meritorious achievement or service to the United States.

description: U.S. Army individual decoration for heroism, meritorious achievement, or meritorious service

description: military rank group
description_wiki: A non-commissioned officer (NCO) is an enlisted leader, petty officer, or in some cases warrant officer, who does not hold a commission.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.
